case_title: GOLDEN GATE REPORTING A CORPORATION VS. WAUKEEN Q MCCOY et al

ANSWER TO CROSS COMPLAINT FILED BY CROSS DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE REPORTING A CORPORATION

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE REPORTING, A CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS, FORM INTERROGATORIES, SPECIALLY PREPARED INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION, AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS - GRANTED IN PART. MOTION TO COMPEL GRANTED. DEFENDANT TO PROVIDE VERIFIED FURTHER RESPONSES WITHIN 30 DAYS TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS, SET NO.1, PLAINTIFF'S FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET NO.1, PLAINTIFF'S SPECIALLY PREPARED INTERROGATORIES, SET NO. 1, AND PLAINTIFF'S DEMAND FOR IDENTIFICATION AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS FOR INSPECTION AND COPYING, SET NO.1. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS DENIED. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; NOT REPORTED

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LAW OFFICE OF WAUKEEN MCCOY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER OVERRULED, TEN DAYS LEAVE TO ANSWER. THE COMPLAINT STATES A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR MONEY DUE FOR SERVICES RENDERED. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER AND RELATED ATTACHMENTS DO NOT SHOW THAT THIS CLAIM IS BARRED AS A MATTER OF LAW. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) Ntc Of Mtn For Order Compelling Further Responses To Req.'S For Admissions, Form Interrogatories, Specially Prepared Interrogatories And Req. For Production, And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-08-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE REPORTING, A CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS, FORM INTERROGATORIES, SPECIALLY PREPARED INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION, AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Granted in part. Motion to compel granted. Defendant to provide verified further responses within 30 days to plaintiff's request for admissions, set no.1, plaintiff's form interrogatories, set no.1, plaintiff's specially prepared interrogatories, set no. 1, and plaintiff's demand for identification and production of documents for inspection and copying, set no.1. Request for sanctions denied. Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Not reported

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-01-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT LAW OFFICE OF WAUKEEN MCCOY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer overruled, ten days leave to answer. The complaint states a cause of action for money due for services rendered. Defendant's demurrer and related attachments do not show that this claim is barred as a matter of law. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
